problem.title: Implement a Queue using an Array
problem.description: Implement a queue data structure using an array with proper enqueue, dequeue, and peek operations.

example.explanation: FIFO: First In First Out. Enqueue adds to back, dequeue removes from front.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Circular Array Implementation [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use a fixed-capacity array with front and rear pointers that wrap around using modulo arithmetic, turning the linear array into a logical circle. This avoids the wasted space that results from a naive front-pointer shift, keeping all operations O(1). The key insight is that tracking a separate size counter makes it easy to distinguish an empty queue from a full one without sacrificing any capacity.
